text:
Olympic heavyweight hero Emily Campbell has slammed gym brands for their lack of size-inclusive gear, revealing she struggles to find kit in her size in shops.

Since snatching a silver at the Tokyo Olympics three years back in the +87kg category, the 30-year-old icon has pumped up her status as a plus-size trailblazer. Fast forward toParis Olympics 2024and Campbell grabbed Team GB's closing piece of shiny hardware with a bronze in the women's +81kg weightlifting class.

Hitting the platform with a hefty total of 288kg made up of 126kg in the snatch and a whopping 162kg clean and jerk, the British powerhouse rounded off her performance with a spectacular cartwheel on the stage. Off the weights though — she's putting the pressure on brands, demanding they step up their game so more women like her can flex their muscles without fitting-room fails.

READ MORE:'I'm an Olympic rugby star and I still have cellulite - it's completely normal'

During an interview on 'Great Company' podcast hosted byMade in ChelseastarJamie Laing, Campbell lifted the lid on her shopping struggles. She explained: "I'm a plus-size female, you know, very proud to be a plus-size female as well. Had to create this body to do my sport most effectively.

"I wasn't this big when I first started weightlifting. I used to be able to walk in a shop and buy clothes. I can't walk in a shop and buy clothes. Now, gym kit stops at size 16, size 16. I'm thinking, well, I'm a size 22-24. Where's my gym kit? All of a sudden I'm having to wear men's horrible baggy T-shirts that don't fit me or whatever."

The influencer left internet users buzzing with her epic clap-back at the fashion industry, saying she's baffled by how bigger women are expected to hit the gym without having the right gear. "Fitness is for everyone, and everyone looks different. We talk about how important that is so let's be inclusive with everyone," she told her followers.

Want all the biggest Lifestyle news straight to your inbox?Sign up for our free Daily Star Hot Topics newsletter.

She added that a brand wanted her to promote them but they didn't go up to her size and any clothes she would wear would have to be specially made. But she said she didn't want sponsorship from them, she just wanted them to "do the right thing" and "make bigger clothes for girls to be able to fit into."

TikTokusers praised her for being a role model for plus-size women. One user said: "Yes, this is true! Companies need to sort this out, there a need for sportswear in larger sizes."

Another wrote: "What an articulate lady. Role model right there," while someone else penned: "This! I started lifting because I watched Emily at the 2020 Olympics and finally saw a body like mine and was in awe."

Listen to the full episode of theGreat Company podcast on Spotify here. New episodes are released every Tuesday.

title: 'I'm an Olympic medalist - I can't buy sportswear in-store because I'm plus-size' - Daily Star
author: Kirsty Card
description: British weightlifter Emily Campbell has slammed gym brands for not being size-inclusive, as she claims she can't find her size in store as a plus-size woman
